title: Add interoperability verification coverage and workflow alignment documentation
summary: This ticket is complete. Release-facing interoperability documentation was added in docs/v0.5.0/interoperability.md, README now links to that guide, and verification coverage was extended for the exposed helper/tool surface so the v0.5.0 workflow contract is documented as one coherent model across rules, scaffold, runtime semantics, bootstrap state expectations, version surfaces, commit gating, file-intent helpers, and the protocol-versus-convention boundary.
outputs: Verification coverage for workflow-critical client/server interoperability behavior, including helper/tool surface exposure relevant to the supported workflow contract
Release-facing documentation that explains the supported lifecycle, file-intent, commit, bootstrap, version, and ticket-workflow contract for v0.5.0
Evidence that mandatory workflow requirements are either server-supported or explicitly documented as convention
A release-facing explanation of how rules, generated bootstrap behavior, and runtime semantics align for the supported v0.5.0 workflow
acceptance_criteria: Tests or equivalent verification checks cover workflow-critical alignment between repository rules and generated initialization rules for the sections treated as protocol-critical in v0.5.0.
Verification coverage confirms the documented canonical lifecycle model for start, progress/update, verify, commit, and terminal completion as exposed to clients.
Verification coverage confirms commit is blocked when verify has not passed and when there are no changes to commit.
Verification coverage confirms supported ergonomic file-intent helper workflows preserve canonical ordering and validation guarantees.

Verification coverage confirms bootstrap-generated state and runtime-expected state remain aligned closely enough for predictable client reasoning, or documentation explicitly states the intended normalized differences.
Verification coverage or release-facing documentation confirms version surfaces remain clear and non-misleading across release, server/package, and schema contexts.
Verification coverage confirms clients can complete all mandatory supported v0.5.0 workflow steps using server-supported primitives without relying on undocumented behavior.
Because ticket persistence is treated as convention rather than protocol, the documentation states that boundary explicitly and consistently.
Release-facing documentation explains what is enforced by the server, what is provided as helper behavior, and what remains client-side operating convention.
Repository verification passed with the interoperability coverage and documentation updates in place.
tasks: Review the final v0.5.0 plan and completed implementation tickets to identify which workflow requirements are protocol-enforced versus documented convention.

Confirm existing verification coverage for workflow-critical alignment between repository rules, generated initialization rules, lifecycle behavior, commit gating, file-intent helper invariants, bootstrap normalization, and version-surface clarity.
Extend verification coverage for the exposed helper/tool surface relevant to the supported workflow contract.
Document the final v0.5.0 interoperability model, including lifecycle semantics, file-intent helper mapping, commit policy, bootstrap/version consistency, and the protocol-versus-convention boundary.
Update release-facing docs so operators and client implementers can understand which behaviors are guaranteed by the server and which remain external workflow discipline.
Run repository verification and capture concise evidence that interoperability coverage and documentation remain consistent with the implemented workflow.
notes: This ticket closes the loop on v0.5.0 by documenting the final client/server contract in release-facing form rather than introducing another major workflow redesign.
The release-facing interoperability guide is provided in docs/v0.5.0/interoperability.md and is linked from README.

The guide explains the boundary between protocol-enforced behavior, supported helper behavior, and client-side convention, including the fact that ticket artifacts remain convention rather than mandatory server protocol.

Verification evidence for lifecycle, commit gating, file-intent invariants, bootstrap normalization, rules/scaffold alignment, and version-surface clarity is provided by the existing and extended test coverage.
The goal of this ticket is to ensure the documented workflow and the implemented workflow are observably the same thing for the supported v0.5.0 surface.
